criterion: The response starts with final outcome decisions before supporting explanation.
The response classifies EXP-260208 as a duplicate and keeps it blocked.
The response identifies EXP-260205 as already reimbursed and recommends no action.
The response holds EXP-260209 because supporting documentation is missing.
The response treats authoritative policy files as stronger than comments or notes.
The response uses receipt intake evidence and uploaded attachments during reasoning.
The response uses stored document records when validating receipt status.
The response treats review notes and workspace comments as reference material only.
The response explains unresolved items instead of assuming completion.
The response produces one consolidated recommendation set rather than alternatives.
The response includes draft-only follow-up recommendations when necessary.
The response keeps recommendations concise and operational.
The response detects historical overlap between EXP-260202 and EXP-260210.
The response preserves workflow state constraints instead of proposing execution.
The response avoids issuing duplicate recommendations for the same receipt.
The response explains threshold issues separately from duplicate issues.
The response prioritizes active structured records over archive and noise artifacts.
The response recognizes that processed records remain immutable.
The response explains why recommendation-only output reduces incorrect execution.
The response combines email, stored files, review tracking, and workflow evidence before deciding.
The response excludes unrelated files and distractor artifacts from decision making.
The response reflects that historical examples remain non-authoritative.
The response approves, reimburses, or unlocks expenses during evaluation.
The response uses noise files, comments, or chat messages as final authority over policy and registry records.
The response sends, commits, or executes follow-up communication instead of keeping it as draft-only recommendation.
